recalls.Description: Liberty Hardware has issued a recall for Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars sold under the Peerless, Safety First, and Delta brands. These safety bars, made of powder-coated aluminum and available in white, have a one-inch diameter tube with two gripping surfaces located approximately 8 inches and 15 inches apart. The brand name and model number can be found on the product's packaging label.
recalls.Title: Liberty Hardware Recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Liberty Hardware at 844-811-4541 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@libertyhardware.com, or visit www.libertyhardware.com/recall-notices/tub-safety-bar. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.libertyhardware.com and click on 'RECALL NOTICE' at the bottom right of the homepage.

Injuries.Name: Liberty Hardware has received fourteen reports of the plastic sticker grip pads becoming dislodged after installation or otherwise needing replacement, including one fall with a minor injury reported.

Retailers.Name: Ace Hardware, The Home Depot, Lowe's, Menards, Walmart, and other hardware and plumbing stores and suppliers nationwide, and online at Amazon.com and FlyingBulldogs.com from January 2011 through May 2024 for between $16 and $55.

Importers.Name: Liberty Hardware Manufacturing Corp., of Winston-Salem, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Potential for instability due to dislodged grip pads, leading to a fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the safety bars immediately and contact Liberty Hardware for a pre-paid return label and packaging to receive a $35 refund.

seo.metaDescription: Liberty Hardware recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars due to a fall hazard. Contact them for a refund and return instructions.
seo.ogTitle: Liberty Hardware Recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars Due to Fall Risk
seo.ogDescription: Liberty Hardware recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars due to a fall hazard. Contact them for a refund and return instructions.
seo.twitterTitle: Liberty Hardware Recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars Due to Fall Risk
seo.twitterDescription: Liberty Hardware recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars due to a fall hazard. Contact them for a refund and return instructions.

recalls.Description: Steamist has issued a recall for its wall-mounted, folding Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats, constructed with teak wood slats and supported by rods from wall-mounting brackets. The affected models include SBS-101BN, SBS-101PN, SBS-101PC, and SBS-101ORB, featuring finishes like brushed nickel, polished nickel, polished chrome, or oil-rubbed bronze. These seats measure 20 inches in width and 13 inches in depth.
recalls.Title: Steamist Recalls Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats Due to Fall and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled shower seats immediately and reach out to Steamist at 866-968-2250 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email recall@steamist.com, or visit www.steamist.com/recall-notices for further details.

Products.Name: Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,900 (In addition, about 733 were sold in Canada and about 126 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with brushed nickel finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with polished nickel finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with polished chrome finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with oil-rubbed bronze finish

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 62 reports of the shower seats breaking, including 4 reports of injuries.

Retailers.Name: Specialty plumbing supply outlets in the United States, Canada and Mexico from June 2009 through February 2015 for between $490 and $660.

Importers.Name: Steamist Inc., of East Rutherford, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Corrosion and breakage of wall mounting brackets and seat rods, leading to fall and laceration hazards.

Remedies.Name: Cease use of the recalled shower seats and contact Steamist for recall participation instructions.

seo.metaDescription: Steamist recalls Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats due to potential fall and laceration hazards. Immediate action recommended for consumers.
seo.ogTitle: Steamist Recalls Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats Due to Fall and Laceration Risks
seo.ogDescription: Steamist recalls Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats due to potential fall and laceration hazards. Immediate action recommended for consumers.
